commit.message:
Fix SQL injection in search by using parameterized queries (#41)

* Fix SQL injection in search by using parameterized queries

The research list search endpoint used string interpolation to build SQL
WHERE clauses, which is vulnerable to SQL injection even with manual
quote escaping. This replaces the string interpolation with proper
parameterized queries via workers-qb's built-in parameter binding.

Extracts a `buildSearchFilters` helper that returns parameterized
conditions and params, and adds comprehensive unit tests including
SQL injection attack scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Adds patch changeset required by CI for the parameterized query fix.

commit.message:
Fix excluded domains not matching when entered as URLs or with www prefix (#43)

Users entering excluded domains as full URLs (e.g. "https://reddit.com")
or with www prefix (e.g. "www.reddit.com") would silently fail to filter
results because the domain comparison expected bare hostnames like
"reddit.com".

Add normalizeDomain() utility that extracts the bare hostname from any
input format. Also strip www. from search result hostnames during
matching for consistency.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add backoff delay before rate limit fallback retries (#39)

When the Gemini API returns a rate limit error, the code now waits
before retrying with the fallback model. The delay is extracted from
the error message when available (e.g. "retry in Xs"), otherwise
defaults to 5 seconds. This prevents the fallback request from also
being rate-limited when requests are made in quick succession.

Also broadens rate limit detection to recognize "429", "Too Many
Requests", and "rate limit" error patterns in addition to the
existing "exceeded your current quota" check.

Relates to #9 and #33.

commit.message:
Fix incorrect null check in re-run and delete routes (#37)

The /re-run and /delete routes checked `!resp` instead of `!resp.results`
to detect missing research entries. Since D1QB always returns a response
object, `!resp` is never true, so the 404 check never triggers. This
causes the code to proceed with null/undefined `resp.results`, crashing
the request when accessing properties on it.

All other routes in the file correctly check `!resp.results`.

commit.message:
Fix learnings duplication in recursive deepResearch calls (#36)

The deepResearch function passes allLearnings to recursive calls as
initialLearnings. The recursive call starts its own allLearnings with
[...initialLearnings], so its returned result already contains the
parent's learnings. Concatenating with spread (...) duplicates them.

This caused exponential growth of the learnings array at each depth
level, wasting tokens when generating the final report and potentially
hitting API token limits sooner.

Fix: replace allLearnings/allUrls with the recursive result (which is
already a superset) instead of concatenating.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix off-by-one error in timeAgo interval labels (#32)

The interval labels in the timeAgo function were shifted one position
early, causing incorrect time unit display. For example, a 90-second
difference would show "1 second ago" instead of "1 minute ago", and
a 2-hour difference would show "2 minutes ago" instead of "2 hours ago".

The labels now correctly correspond to the unit produced after dividing
by each interval. Also removed the unnecessary POSITIVE_INFINITY sentinel
entry, and added comprehensive tests for both timeAgo and formatDuration.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Display research history for completed/errored researches (#28)

* feat: Display research history for completed/errored researches

Adds a collapsible section to the research details page to display the research history.

This section is only visible when the research is completed or in an error state.
The history is fetched from the `research_status_history` table and displayed using the `ResearchStatusHistoryDisplay` component.
The section is collapsed by default.

* feat: Display full research history in a compact view

This commit introduces two main changes:

1.  Fetches all research history entries for completed (status 2) or errored (status 3) researches in `src/index.tsx`. Previously, it was limited to the last 5 entries.
2.  Updates the `ResearchStatusHistoryDisplay` component in `src/templates/layout.tsx` to support a compact display mode.
    - A new `isLiveView` prop is added.
    - When `isLiveView` is false (for completed/errored researches), the history is displayed without timestamps and with a smaller font for better readability of longer logs.
    - When `isLiveView` is true (for in-progress researches), the display remains the same, showing timestamps and regular font size for live updates.

These changes allow you to view the complete history of a research job in a more condensed format once it's no longer in a live state.

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Prevent TypeError in report download dropdown (#23)

* Fix: Prevent TypeError in report download dropdown

The onClick handler for the report download button was attempting to access the classList property of this.nextElementSibling without checking if it existed. This caused a TypeError when the button was the last child of its parent.

This commit updates the onClick handler to check if this.nextElementSibling exists before attempting to access its classList property, preventing the error.

* Fix: Prevent TypeError in report download dropdown

The onClick handler for the report download button was attempting to access the classList property of this.nextElementSibling without checking if it existed. This caused a TypeError when the button was the last child of its parent.

This commit updates the onClick handler to check if this.nextElementSibling exists before attempting to access its classList property, preventing the error.

* Fix: Dropdown toggle by ID

This commit fixes an issue where the report download dropdown was not
functioning correctly.

The previous fix attempt using this.nextElementSibling failed because
`this` was undefined in the onClick handler's context.

This commit implements the following changes:
- Assigns a unique dynamic ID to the dropdown menu div (e.g., `download-dropdown-${id}`).
- Updates the onClick handler on the "Download Report" button to directly
  use `document.getElementById()` with this unique ID to find and toggle
  the visibility of the dropdown menu.

This ensures a more robust way of targeting and manipulating the dropdown
element, independent of its direct sibling relationship or the context of `this`.

* Fix: Dropdown toggle by ID

This commit fixes an issue where the report download dropdown was not
functioning correctly.

The previous fix attempt using this.nextElementSibling failed because
`this` was undefined in the onClick handler's context.

This commit implements the following changes:
- Assigns a unique dynamic ID to the dropdown menu div (e.g., `download-dropdown-${id}`).
- Updates the onClick handler on the "Download Report" button to directly
  use `document.getElementById()` with this unique ID to find and toggle
  the visibility of the dropdown menu.

This ensures a more robust way of targeting and manipulating the dropdown
element, independent of its direct sibling relationship or the context of `this`.

* Fix: Dropdown toggle with dedicated JS function

This commit resolves the "toggleDropdown is not defined" ReferenceError and ensures the report download dropdown functions correctly.

The previous approaches had issues with either `this` context in inline handlers or premature submission before all parts of the solution were in place.

This commit implements the following:
1. Modifies the `onClick` handler on the "Download Report" button in `src/index.tsx` to call a new JavaScript function `toggleDropdown(id)`, passing the research `id`.

commit.message: Fix research history not cascading
Fix model fallback

Add live research status tracking and display (#16)

* Add live research status tracking and display

This commit introduces a live loading status feature for the report details page, providing you with real-time updates on my progress.

Key changes:

1.  **Database Schema:**
    *   Added a new table `research_status_history` to store timestamped status entries for each research task.
    *   Includes columns: `id`, `research_id`, `timestamp`, `status_text`.

2.  **Backend API for Status:**
    *   Created a new route `/research/:id/status` that returns a partial HTML snippet containing the latest (up to 10) status history entries for a given research ID.

3.  **Frontend Display with HTMX:**
    *   On the `ResearchDetails` page, when a research task is "Processing":
        *   An HTMX component now polls the `/research/:id/status` endpoint every 5 seconds.
        *   The fetched status history is dynamically displayed, showing what I am currently doing (e.g., "Crawling URL: ...", "Searching query: ...").
        *   A loading icon (spinner) is shown next to the main "Processing" status.
        *   An HTMX indicator provides feedback during polling updates ("Fetching latest status...").
        *   Each historical status entry is now displayed with a small icon and timestamp for clarity.

4.  **Workflow Integration:**
    *   The `ResearchWorkflow` and associated functions (`deepResearch`, `webSearch`) have been updated to log status updates to the `research_status_history` table at various key stages:
        *   Workflow initiation and completion/failure.
        *   Generation of SERP queries.
        *   Execution of web searches for specific queries.
        *   Crawling of individual URLs.
        *   Processing of search results.

This feature enhances your experience by providing transparency into the research process, similar to other deep research tools.

* Fix live updates

* Fix: Improve research history and details page UI/UX (#17)

This commit addresses several UI/UX issues on the research history and details pages:

1.  **Filter Status Messages:**
    - Messages "Generating SERP queries for research" and "processing serp results" are now filtered out from the live status history display on the research details page. This is achieved by preventing these messages from being saved to the database in the `addResearchStatusHistoryEntry` function.

2.  **Collapse Research Context by Default:**
    - The "Research Context" section (containing the initial query and follow-up questions) on the research details page is now collapsed by default to provide a cleaner initial view. You can expand it by clicking.

3.  **Update Live Status Display:**
    - The number of live status updates shown on the research details page is now limited to the latest 5 entries (previously 10).
    - The visual styling of the status history has been modernized:
        - Increased padding and added a hover effect to list items.
        - Changed the icon to a filled circle for a cleaner look.
        - Improved typography and spacing for better readability.

These changes enhance your experience by making the research details page less cluttered and the status updates more focused and visually appealing. I've ensured all changes function as expected.

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix live updates

* Refactor: Embed live updates in research details page (#19)

This commit refactors the research details page to embed live status updates directly within the page, similar to the research index page's live reload functionality.

Key changes:

- Modified `src/index.tsx`:
    - The `/details/:id` route now handles a `partial=true` query parameter. If true, it renders only the `ResearchDetails` component; otherwise, it renders the full layout.
    - Fetches `statusHistory` and passes it along with an `isPartial` flag to the `ResearchDetails` component.
    - Removed the separate `/research/:id/status` endpoint, as its functionality is now integrated into the details page.
    - Removed the `<script>loadResearchDetails()</script>` as HTMX now handles updates.

- Modified `src/templates/layout.tsx`:
    - Updated the `ResearchDetails` component to accept `statusHistory` and `isPartial` props.
    - The component now uses HTMX to poll `/details/:id?partial=true` for updates if the research is in a processing state and the page is not a partial render.
    - The live status update display (using `ResearchStatusHistoryDisplay`) has been moved into the main content area, below the research context section and above the report body.
    - The old HTMX attributes for fetching status from the separate endpoint were removed.

This change streamlines the live update mechanism for the research details page, making it more consistent with other parts of the application and removing an unnecessary endpoint.

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Correct HTMX partial update in ResearchDetails (#20)

This commit fixes the HTMX mechanism for live updates on the research details page. Previously, HTMX was configured to swap the `innerHTML` of an inner div with the entire `<main>` component returned by the partial request, leading to incorrect rendering (nested <main> tags).

The correction involves:
- Modifying `src/templates/layout.tsx` for the `ResearchDetails` component:
    - The `<main>` tag of `ResearchDetails` is now assigned a dynamic ID (e.g., `research-details-main-XYZ`).
    - If live polling is active (research status is 'processing' and it's not a partial render), the HTMX attributes (`hx-get`, `hx-trigger`, `hx-target`, `hx-swap`, `hx-indicator`) are applied directly to this `<main>` tag.
    - `hx-target` now points to the `<main>` tag's own ID.
    - `hx-swap` is set to `outerHTML`.
- This ensures that when the partial content is fetched (which is the full `ResearchDetails` component, i.e., the `<main>` tag and its content), the entire existing `<main>` tag is correctly replaced, preventing rendering issues.
- The intermediate wrapper div previously used for HTMX targeting has been removed as it's no longer necessary.

This change ensures the live update feature on the research details page functions correctly by properly replacing the component content during polling.

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix/details refresh (#21)

* Fix: Details page auto-refresh continues after first update

The research details page was only auto-refreshing once while a research task was processing. This was due to the HTMX polling attributes not being included in partial updates.

The `ResearchDetails` component in `src/templates/layout.tsx` conditionally adds HTMX attributes for polling based on the research status. The condition previously included a check `!researchData.isPartial` which prevented these attributes from being added to the response of a partial update (which the polling mechanism uses).

This commit removes the `!researchData.isPartial` part of the condition. Now, the polling attributes are included as long as `researchData.status === 1` (processing), ensuring that the page continues to refresh every 5 seconds until the research is complete or fails.

* The research details page now only renders the `report_html` content when the research status is 'Completed' (status code 2).

This prevents displaying an empty or incomplete report section while the research is still processing or if it has failed. The `ResearchDetails` component in `src/templates/layout.tsx` was updated to include a conditional check around the report rendering block.

* Fix live updates

commit.message:
feat: Implement auto-reloading research list (#15)

* feat: Implement auto-reloading research list

Adds htmx attributes to the ResearchList component to enable auto-reloading.

The component now fetches the current page HTML every 10 seconds and
replaces its content with the response, providing an auto-reloading
experience without a full browser refresh.

The `hx-get` attribute dynamically uses the current page URL,
including any pagination parameters.

* feat: Implement partial auto-reloading for research list

I've modified the research list page to support efficient auto-reloading
using htmx and server-side partial rendering.

- Server-side: The main route handler (`src/index.tsx`) now checks
  for a `partial=true` query parameter. If present, it renders
  only the `ResearchList` component's HTML, excluding the main layout.
- Client-side: The `ResearchList` component (`src/templates/layout.tsx`)
  now includes `partial=true` in its htmx `hx-get` requests for
  polling. It uses `hx-swap="outerHTML"` to replace itself with the
  fetched partial content every 10 seconds.

This approach ensures that only the necessary fragment of the page
is transferred and updated, providing a smoother and more efficient
auto-reloading experience without full page refreshes.

* Fix model fallback

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* I've made a correction to the fallback model name.

This update addresses the fallback model name used in the research workflow. Based on your feedback, the fallback model has been changed from "gemini-1.0-pro" to "gemini-2.0-flash".

Here's what changed:

- I also updated tests in `src/workflows.test.ts` to reflect the corrected fallback model name in mock configurations and assertions.

All tests continue to pass with this correction.

* ci: Add GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing

This commit introduces a GitHub Actions workflow to automate the execution
of unit tests. The workflow is defined in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

The CI pipeline will:
- Trigger on every push and pull_request to any branch.
- Use a Node.js environment (version 20.x).
- Checkout the code.
- Install dependencies using `npm ci`.
- Run the test suite using `npm test`.

This will help ensure that all tests pass before merging changes,
improving code quality and stability.

- I updated `getFallbackModel` in `src/utils.ts` to return the "gemini-2.0-flash" model.

All tests continue to pass with this correction.

of unit tests. The workflow is defined in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

The CI pipeline will:
- Trigger on every push and pull_request to any branch.
- Use a Node.js environment (version 20.x).
- Checkout the code.
- Install dependencies using `npm ci`.
- Run the test suite using `npm test`.

This will help ensure that all tests pass before merging changes,
improving code quality and stability.

These changes aim to provide you with a clearer understanding of the project's capabilities and internal workings. * Fix: Correct and simplify Mermaid diagrams in README This commit addresses feedback on the Mermaid diagrams in README.md: 1. **Simplified System Architecture Chart:** * Removed frontend-specific components (User Browser, Hono JSX Dashboard) from the main system architecture diagram to reduce complexity and focus on backend interactions. * Updated the subgraph title to "Cloudflare Ecosystem (Backend)". 2. **Corrected Research Workflow Chart:** * Fixed a parsing error that occurred during rendering on GitHub by replacing HTML `
` tags within node labels with newline characters (` `). * Ensured multi-line labels are enclosed in double quotes for correct Mermaid syntax. These changes improve the accuracy and readability of the diagrams, ensuring they render correctly and provide a clear overview of the project's architecture and workflow. * Refactor: Heavily simplify Research Workflow Diagram in README This commit addresses your feedback to heavily simplify the Research Workflow Diagram in README.md. Error handling flows, including the 'ERR' node and related edges, have been removed from the Mermaid chart. The diagram now focuses exclusively on the sequence of operations from your input to successful research completion and report storage. --------- Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Use server-side endpoint for report downloads

This commit refactors the report download functionality to use a dedicated
server-side endpoint instead of client-side JavaScript. This approach is
more robust and allows for easier extension to other download formats
in the future.

Changes include:
- Added a new GET route `/details/:id/download/markdown` in `src/index.tsx`.
  This route handles fetching the report data and returning the raw
  markdown content with appropriate `Content-Type` and
  `Content-Disposition` headers to trigger a browser download.
- Updated the "Download Report" button on the report details page to be
  an `<a>` tag pointing to this new endpoint. The `download` attribute
  is used to suggest the filename "report.md".
- Removed the now-unused `downloadReport` JavaScript function from
  `src/static/core.js`.

feat: Implement report download as markdown

This commit introduces a "Download Report" button on the report details page.
When clicked, this button triggers the download of the report content as a markdown file named "report.md".

The changes include:
- Added a "Download Report" button to the `TopBar` in `src/index.tsx` for the report details view.
- Implemented a JavaScript function `downloadReport(filename, content)` in `src/static/core.js` to handle the file creation and download trigger.
- Connected the button's `onClick` event to this JavaScript function, passing the report's raw markdown content.
- Verified that `core.js` is included in the main layout.

feat: Implement initial context for deep research (#11)

* feat: Implement initial context for deep research

This commit introduces a feature that allows you to provide initial data/learnings when creating a new deep research project.

Key changes:

- Added an optional "Initial Learnings" textarea to the new research page.
- Updated the `ResearchType` and `ResearchTypeDB` types to include an `initialLearnings` field.
- Modified the `CreateResearch` component in `src/templates/layout.tsx` to include the new textarea and pass its value during form submission.
- Updated `src/static/core.js` to handle the new field in the form.
- Modified the `ResearchWorkflow` in `src/workflows.ts` to:
    - Receive `initialLearnings` from the event payload.
    - Split the `initialLearnings` string by newlines.
    - Pass the resulting array to the `deepResearch` function to prefill the learnings.

This allows you to seed the research process with existing knowledge, potentially leading to more focused and relevant reports.

* feat: Implement initial context and rerun for deep research

This commit introduces a feature that allows you to provide initial data/learnings when creating a new deep research project and ensures these learnings are carried over when a project is rerun.

Key changes:

- Added an optional "Initial Learnings" textarea to the new research page.
- Updated `src/types.ts`: Added `initialLearnings` to `ResearchType` and `ResearchTypeDB`.
- Modified `src/templates/layout.tsx` and `src/static/core.js`: Included the new textarea in `CreateResearch` and updated form handling.
- Modified `src/workflows.ts`: The `ResearchWorkflow` now processes `initialLearnings` from the payload and uses them to seed the research.
- Added a database migration to `src/migrations.ts` (`0004_add_initial_learnings_column.sql`) to add an `initialLearnings` TEXT column to the `researches` table.
- Updated `src/index.tsx`:
    - The `/create` endpoint now extracts `initialLearnings` from form data, saves it to the DB, and passes it to the workflow.
    - The `/re-run` endpoint now fetches `initialLearnings` from the original research and passes them to the new workflow and saves them for the new research record. Title is also carried over.

This allows you to seed the research process with existing knowledge and maintain that context when rerunning, potentially leading to more focused and relevant reports.

* feat: Display initial learnings in research details

This commit updates the ResearchDetails component to display the
initial learnings you provided, if any.

- Modified `src/templates/layout.tsx`:
    - The `ResearchDetails` component now conditionally renders an
      "Initial Learnings" section below the "Initial Query".
    - Learnings are displayed line-by-line for readability.
- Verified that `src/index.tsx` correctly fetches and passes
  `initialLearnings` to the `ResearchDetails` component.

This enhances the usability of the research details page by providing
full context of the inputs used for the research.

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

readme_html:
workers-research

A serverless, Cloudflare Workers-based Deep Research Agent powered by Google Gemini 2.5.

✨ Features

  • In-depth Research with Google Gemini 2.5: Leverages Google's latest models for high-quality research reports.
  • Serverless on Cloudflare: Built with Workers and Workflows for scalability, reliability, and cost-effectiveness.
  • Intuitive Dashboard: Manage and view research via a Hono/JSX web interface.
  • Simplified Architecture: Gemini 2.5's large context window removes the need for prompt compression, enhancing report quality.
  • AutoRAG Integration: Allows the system to perform research using Cloudflare AI's AutoRAG feature, which queries against a pre-indexed dataset. This can be used as an alternative or in conjunction with standard web browsing research. The AutoRAG feature is invoked when an autorag_id is provided with the research request.

🚀 Inspiration and Acknowledgements

Inspired by dzhng's deep-research. Thank you to dzhng for the original concept. workers-research adapts this for the Cloudflare ecosystem.

💡 Key Differences from Original Deep-Research

  • Cloudflare Native: Runs on Cloudflare Workers and Workflows, unlike the Node.js/Docker original.
  • Enhanced Reliability: Uses Cloudflare Workflows for robust multi-step research.
  • Simplified & Efficient: Leverages Gemini 2.5's large context window, omitting prompt compression for a streamlined design and potentially better reports.
  • Accessible Crawling: Utilizes Cloudflare's Browser Rendering (available on the free Workers plan).

🔍 Use Cases

  • Academic Research: Quickly gather information and insights for academic papers, theses, and research projects.
  • Market Analysis: Research industry trends, competitors, and market dynamics for business decisions.
  • Content Creation: Generate comprehensive research for blog posts, articles, and educational content.
  • Product Development: Explore user needs, technical solutions, and industry standards for new products.
  • Educational Projects: Help students gather information on complex topics for projects and assignments.

🛠️ Technology Stack

  • Cloudflare Workers: Core runtime environment.
  • Cloudflare Workflows: Manages reliable research execution.
  • Cloudflare D1 Database: Serverless SQL for research data.
  • Browser Rendering: Web crawling (free tier available).
  • Google Gemini 2.5: Advanced language model for reports.
  • Cloudflare AI Gateway (Optional): Proxy for AI requests (caching, management).
  • Hono: Lightweight web framework for dashboard and API.
  • workers-qb: Query builder for Cloudflare D1.

🏗️ Architecture Overview

graph TD
    B{Cloudflare Worker} -- API Calls --> D[Cloudflare Workflows];
    B -- Fetches Status/Data --> F[Cloudflare D1 Database];
    D -- Triggers --> E[Gemini 2.5 API];
    D -- Stores/Retrieves Research --> F;
    D -- If Crawling Needed --> G[Browser Rendering API];
    E -- Report Chunks/Results --> D;

    subgraph "Cloudflare Ecosystem (Backend)"
        B
        D
        F
        G
    end

    subgraph "External Services"
        E
    end

    style B fill:#ccf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    style D fill:#ccf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    style E fill:#fca,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    style F fill:#ccf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    style G fill:#ccf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;

Research Workflow Diagram

The following diagram illustrates the detailed workflow of the research agent when processing a request:

graph TD
    A["User Input: Query, Parameters"] --> B["System: Prepare Full Query & Setup"];
    B --> C["Start Iterative Research Cycle"];
    C --> C1["Generate Search Tasks/Queries based on current goal & learnings"];
    C1 --> C2["Gather Information (Web Search & Content Extraction)"];
    C2 --> C3["Process Information: Extract Key Learnings & Identify Follow-up Needs"];
    C3 --> C4["Further Research Iterations Needed? (based on depth/breadth)"];
    C4 -- Yes --> C1;
    C4 -- No --> D["Compile All Learnings & Generate Final Report"];
    D --> E["Store Report & Update Status in Database"];
    E --> F["Research Complete / Report Available"];

🚦 Getting Started

Follow these steps to set up and run workers-research on your Cloudflare account:

Setup Steps

  1. Clone the Repository:

    git clone git@github.com:G4brym/workers-research.git

  2. Install Dependencies:

    npm install

  3. Cloudflare Login: Authenticate with your Cloudflare account using Wrangler:

    wrangler login

  4. Create a Cloudflare D1 Database: Create a D1 database in your Cloudflare dashboard or using Wrangler:

    wrangler d1 create workers-research

    Important: Replace "replace-me" in your wrangler.toml file with the database_id output from the command above.

    Database migrations are applied automatically.

  5. Deploy to Cloudflare Workers: Deploy your worker to Cloudflare:

    wrangler deploy

    This will deploy your worker to workers-research.{your-user}.workers.dev (or your custom domain if configured).

  6. Set up API Keys:

    • Google AI Studio API Key: Obtain from https://aistudio.google.com.
    • Upload API Keys into workers: 
      npx wrangler secret put GOOGLE_API_KEY

  7. (Optional) Configure Cloudflare AI Gateway:

    • Create an AI Gateway in your Cloudflare account dashboard.
    • Configure the gateway with your preferred settings.
    • Set the required environment variables: 
      npx wrangler secret put AI_GATEWAY_NAME
npx wrangler secret put AI_GATEWAY_ACCOUNT_ID
    • If your gateway has authentication enabled, also set the API key: 
      npx wrangler secret put AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY

  8. Access the Dashboard: Open your deployed worker URL (e.g., https://workers-research.{your-user}.workers.dev) in your browser to access the research dashboard.

✍️ Usage

  1. Create a New Research:

    • On the dashboard homepage, you'll see a "Create New Research" section.
    • Enter your research query, desired depth, and breadth.
    • Click "Continue with creation".

  2. Answer Follow-up Questions:

    • The application will generate follow-up questions to clarify your research intent.
    • Answer these questions to refine the research direction.
    • Click "Create new Research" to start the research workflow.

  3. Monitor Research Status:

    • You can track the status of your researches on the dashboard.
    • Researches in progress will be marked as "Running".
    • Completed researches will be marked as "Complete".

  4. Read Research Reports:

    • Once a research is complete, a "Read" button will appear next to it.
    • Click "Read" to view the generated research report in a nicely formatted HTML page.

  5. Re-run Researches:

    • If you want to re-run a previous research, click the "🔄" button next to the research entry. This will create a new research based on the same query and parameters.

📊 Performance & Limitations

  • Research Time: Most researches complete within 5-10 minutes, depending on complexity.
  • Research Depth: Configure depth from 1-5, with higher values producing more comprehensive reports.
  • API Limits: Be aware of Cloudflare and Google Gemini API limits when running multiple researches. Browser Rendering on the Workers Free plan is limited to 10 minutes of usage per day, sufficient for most research tasks.
